title: Stabilization of Zero-Excess <mark>Sodium</mark> Metal Anodes Using LiPF6
authors: Louis V. Morris, James L. Hart, Zoey Warecki, Corey Love, Rachel E. Carter and Gordon H. Waller
abstract: The adoption of <mark>sodium</mark> metal anodes is limited due to their poor <mark>coulombic efficiency</mark> stemming from dendritic plating morphologies and incomplete <mark>solid electrolyte</mark> interface (<mark>SEI</mark>) formation. This study presents a <mark>sodium</mark> metal battery electrolyte formulation utilizing <mark>lithium</mark> hexafluorophosphate as an additive, which selectively decomposes at the <mark>sodium</mark> metal interface to form a highly uniform, compact, and inorganic-rich <mark>SEI</mark> layer. This improved passivation layer enables >99% columbic efficiency operation of zero-excess <mark>sodium</mark> metal anodes. The mechanism of action of this additive is further e…

title: Ex Situ Pre-Lithiation of Silicon-Based Anode Materials
authors: Shiba P. Adhikari, Nicholas P. W. Pieczonka, Sayed Youssef Sayed, Jeffrey D. Cain and Jianlin Li
abstract: The implementation of silicon (Si) and silicon oxide (SiOx) anodes for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) is hindered by first-cycle capacity loss and low <mark>Coulombic efficiency</mark> due to unstable <mark>solid electrolyte</mark> <mark>interphase</mark> (SEI) formation, which consumes <mark>lithium</mark> and leads to poor cycle life. Here, we present an electrochemical continuous stirred tank reactor (ECSTR) as a platform for ex situ pre-lithiation of Si and SiOx anode materials. Unlike conventional techniques, which suffer from poor <mark>lithium</mark> distribution and limited scalability, our ECSTR enables uniform, controlled lithiation of Si-based powd…

title: Grain boundary engineered aluminum current collector for energy-dense initially anode-free <mark>sodium</mark> metal batteries
abstract: Published online: 06 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74177-8 The initially anode-free <mark>sodium</mark> metal offer advantages in <mark>energy density</mark> and <mark>safety</mark>. Here, authors design a grain-boundary Ga-rich polycrystalline aluminum current collector that enables controlled Al3+ dissolution to compensate Na loss, stabilizes Na plating/stripping, and delivers reversible batteries.
